title: 10 best herbal essential oils for nipple pain during breastfeeding
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for nipple pain during breastfeeding are a natural solution to alleviate discomfort and promote healing of sore nipples, which often occur during the early stages of breastfeeding. These oils are derived from plants and can be used topically to soothe and calm the skin. The best herbal essential oils for nipple pain during breastfeeding include Calendula officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, Aloe barbadensis, Echinacea purpurea, Symphytum officinale, Chamomilla recutita, Melaleuca alternifolia, Melissa officinalis, Arnica montana, and Mentha x piperita. If left untreated, nipple pain can lead to decreased milk supply, breastfeeding cessation, and increased stress levels, ultimately affecting a mother's ability to bond with her baby and enjoy the breastfeeding experience.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because it has natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, peppermint oil can help to numb the affected area, reducing the discomfort and soreness associated with nipple thrush or cracked nipples. Additionally, its cooling sensation can promote blood flow and reduce swelling, further alleviating pain and promoting healing. This natural remedy can provide relief for breastfeeding mothers, allowing them to focus on bonding with their newborn instead of suffering through nipple pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint essential oil helps reduce inflammation in the nipples and surrounding breast tissue, which can alleviate pain caused by mastitis or engorgement.
Anesthetic properties: The cooling sensation of peppermint oil can numb the area, providing temporary relief from nipple pain and discomfort during breastfeeding.
Antispasmodic properties: Peppermint oil's ability to relax and calm muscle spasms in the nipples and surrounding tissue can help reduce pain caused by latch issues or nipple thrush.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant are rich in menthol, which helps to numb and soothe nipple pain, making them an effective ingredient in essential oils.
Leaves: Peppermint leaves contain a high concentration of mentone, a natural analgesic that provides relief from nipple soreness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves at dawn when they are most potent and rich in menthol.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 12 hours.
Steam distill the dried peppermint leaves using a steam distillation apparatus with distilled water for 4 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through cheesecloth into dark glass bottles and store in a cool dry place.
Mix 5ml of the extracted peppermint essential oil with 95ml of coconut oil or sweet almond oil for topical application during breastfeeding sessions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because of its calming and soothing properties. Lavender oil contains linalool, a natural analgesic that can help reduce inflammation and ease discomfort in the nipples. When applied topically to the affected area, it can provide instant relief from soreness and tenderness, allowing mothers to breastfeed comfortably. Additionally, lavender oil's calming scent can also promote relaxation and reduce stress levels, making it an effective remedy for addressing nipple pain during breastfeeding.
remedy_properties: Anesthetic and analgesic properties: Lavender essential oil has been shown to have anesthetic and analgesic effects, which can help numb and reduce the sensation of pain in the nipples, providing relief from discomfort and soreness during breastfeeding.

Anti-inflammatory and anti-irritant properties: Lavender essential oil has anti-inflammatory and anti-irritant properties that can help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin around the nipple area, reducing redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with nipple pain.

Calming and relaxing properties: Lavender essential oil is known for its calming and relaxing effects, which can help reduce stress and anxiety related to breastfeeding difficulties or nipple pain, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation that can aid in the healing process.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate nipple soreness.

Flowers: Known for their calming effects on the nervous system, the flowers of Lavandula angustifolia can help reduce stress and promote relaxation, addressing nipple pain associated with breastfeeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning when dew drops are still present on the leaves.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm, dry place for 7-10 days or use a food dehydrator at 60 degrees celsius.
Combine one cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or jojoba oil.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar with a lid for 14-21 days, shaking daily and filtering every 7 days.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding the solids and storing the essential oil in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil's active compounds, such as calendulic acid, have been shown to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort associated with nipple soreness caused by friction and moisture imbalance. Additionally, pot marigold essential oils' gentle astringent properties help to reduce swelling and promote healing, allowing for more comfortable breastfeeding sessions.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Calendula oil's antibacterial properties help combat bacterial infections that can cause nipple pain, redness, and inflammation during breastfeeding.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Calendula oil reduce swelling and irritation, providing relief from sore, cracked, or damaged nipples often experienced by breastfeeding mothers.
Wound-healing: Calendula's wound-healing properties promote the regeneration of new tissue, helping to accelerate the healing process and soothe nipple pain caused by breastfeeding trauma.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of pot marigold contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm sore nipples, reducing discomfort and promoting healing.

Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation, pain, and swelling associated with nipple trauma during breastfeeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers at peak bloom in late summer or early fall for optimal potency and color.
Dry the harvested flowers immediately using a low-temperature dehydrator for 24 hours and then store them in an airtight container.
Use a high-quality solvent like carrier oil such as coconut oil to extract the essential oils from dried calendula petals at a ratio of 1 part calendula to 2 parts carrier oil.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2 weeks shaking the container daily and then filter it through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle.
Store the resulting calendula essential oil in an airtight glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid away from direct sunlight and heat for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose essential oils helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The oil's gentle, non-irritating nature makes it an effective topical solution for relieving soreness and discomfort associated with nipple engorgement, cracks, or thrush infections. Additionally, the calming scent of damask rose has a sedative effect, promoting relaxation and reducing stress that may exacerbate nipple pain, allowing mothers to enjoy a more comfortable and enjoyable breastfeeding experience.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Damask rose essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and discomfort in the nipples, making it an effective remedy for relieving nipple pain caused by engorgement or trauma.

Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic properties of damask rose essential oil help to ease spasms and tension in the milk ducts and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to nipple pain during breastfeeding.

Analgesic property: Damask rose essential oil's analgesic properties provide natural relief from pain and discomfort, helping to numb the area and reduce sensitivity, making it an effective solution for nipple pain caused by friction or irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, roots are often used in herbal remedies to soothe and calm irritated skin, making them suitable for alleviating nipple pain during breastfeeding.

Flowers: The delicate flowers of the damask rose plant contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair, making them a natural remedy for soothing sore nipples.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried rosa damascena flowers at peak blooming season for maximum potency.
Combine the dried flowers with 100 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar for infusion.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and allow it to infuse for 6 weeks shaking daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding solids.
Add 10 milliliters of high-proof glycerin to the liquid extract and stir well to create a therapeutic essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because its anti-inflammatory properties can soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing discomfort and pain. The oil's analgesic and antispasmodic effects can also help to relax the muscles around the nipples, easing tension and pressure that may be contributing to pain. Additionally, Tasmanian blue gum essential oils have been shown to promote wound healing and tissue repair, which can aid in the recovery of any nipple damage caused by breastfeeding.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum oil has anti-inflammatory compounds like eucalyptol and cineole, which can help reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with nipple pain, making it more comfortable for breastfeeding.

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: The essential oil contains compounds that inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, reducing the risk of infection and inflammation that may contribute to nipple pain during breastfeeding.

Pain-relieving and analgesic properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum oil has a soothing effect on skin irritations, helping to alleviate pain and discomfort caused by cracked or sore nipples, making it easier for new mothers to breastfeed without distress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Tasmanian blue gum contain bioactive compounds like eugenol and alpha-pinene, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving nipple soreness and pain during breastfeeding.

Barks: The barks of the plant are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which possess antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, helping to soothe and protect the nipple area from infection and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in the early morning when dew is still present on the leaves.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 95°f for 24 hours or by air-drying them for 7-10 days.
Combine 200g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves with 400ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar twice daily to promote even infusion.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and storing the essential oil in an amber glass bottle at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort essential oils helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because it has natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that soothe and calm irritated nipples. The oil's active compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain caused by nipple thrush, cracks, or damage. When applied topically to the affected area, St John's wort essential oils can provide quick relief from discomfort, making it easier for new mothers to enjoy a peaceful and satisfying breastfeeding experience.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: St. John's Wort essential oil has antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the breast tissue, which can contribute to nipple pain.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of St. John's Wort essential oil help numb the painful sensation, providing immediate relief from sharp shooting pains or discomfort during breastfeeding.

Anti-inflammatory: St. John's Wort essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and redness in the breast tissue, which can help alleviate nipple pain caused by engorgement or thrush.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving nipple pain associated with breastfeeding.
Flowers: The flowers contain a high concentration of hyperforin, a compound known for its ability to reduce inflammation and ease discomfort, making them an excellent choice for soothing nipple pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st. john's wort leaves and flowers at peak flowering stage, typically mid-to-late summer.
Dry the harvested plant material by spreading it out in thin layers for 7-10 days, monitoring for humidity.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder, yielding approximately 100g.
Steam distill the powdered hypericum perforatum for 2 hours at 20-30°c, collecting 10ml of essential oil.
Filter the distilled essential oil through a coffee filter or paper filter to remove impurities and sediment before bottling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood essential oils helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. It reduces swelling and soothes irritated skin, providing instant relief to sore nipples. Additionally, wormwood oil has antimicrobial properties that help to prevent infections, further reducing discomfort and promoting healing. As a result, mothers can enjoy the bonding experience of breastfeeding without the added stress and pain associated with nipple irritation.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Wormwood essential oil helps to relax and calm down spasms in the nipple and breast tissue, reducing discomfort and pain associated with breastfeeding.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Wormwood essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the nipple and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to pain during breastfeeding.

Pain-relieving and anesthetic properties: The analgesic and anesthetic compounds in wormwood essential oil help to numb and relieve pain in the nipple and breast tissue, providing fast and effective relief from discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are valued for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in alleviating nipple pain associated with breastfeeding.
Barks: Wormwood bark is rich in sesquiterpenes, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects that can help soothe nipple pain and discomfort during lactation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried leaves of artemisia absinthium and dry them completely in shade for 7-10 days.
Crush the dried leaves into small pieces using mortar and pestle, taking about 30 minutes to achieve desired consistency.
Place the crushed artemisia absinthium leaves in a glass jar with a tight-fitting lid and cover with carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1 part herb to 4 parts oil.
Store the jar in a dark, cool place for 2-3 weeks, shaking gently every other day to aid infusion process, allowing oils to mingle and extract beneficial compounds.

Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding solids and transferring the infused oil to clean glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for storage and future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated nipples. Its calming effect also reduces stress and anxiety, common contributors to nipple soreness. Additionally, lemon balm's antispasmodic properties ease muscle tension in the breast tissue, alleviating discomfort and making breastfeeding a more enjoyable experience for both mother and baby. As a result, using lemon balm essential oils can promote a smoother, less painful breastfeeding journey.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal lemon balm essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the nipples, making them less tender and painful.
Anxiolytic: Lemon balm's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties can help calm the nerves and promote relaxation, which can help alleviate nipple pain caused by breastfeeding anxiety or stress.
Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic properties of lemon balm essential oil can help relax the muscle tissues around the nipples, reducing cramping and spasms that can cause pain during breastfeeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon balm contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving nipple pain during breastfeeding.
Seeds: Lemon balm seeds are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to soothe and calm the breast tissue, reducing discomfort and inflammation associated with nipple pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves at dawn on a dry day weighing approximately 100 grams per batch.
Clean the harvested leaves by gently brushing off dirt and debris using a soft-bristled brush.
Steam distill the cleaned leaves in a water bath at 100 degrees celsius for 30 minutes to extract the essential oil.
Mix the distilled essential oil with a carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a ratio of 2% essential oil to 98% carrier oil.
Store the final herbal melissa officinalis essential oil blend in dark glass bottles and use within six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The gentle, calming scent of chamomile also promotes relaxation, reducing anxiety and stress that can exacerbate nipple discomfort. When applied topically or inhaled, chamomile oil reduces inflammation, eases tension, and promotes healing, providing instant relief from soreness and discomfort, allowing mothers to focus on nurturing their baby without distraction.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and discomfort caused by sore or damaged nipples, promoting healing and relief from pain.

Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic properties of chamomile essential oil help to relax the muscles in the breast tissue and nipple area, reducing spasms and discomfort that can contribute to pain during breastfeeding.

Calming and soothing property: Chamomile essential oil's calming and soothing properties have a direct impact on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and stress related to breastfeeding difficulties, which can exacerbate nipple pain.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of the chamomile plant are a popular choice for making essential oils, as they contain high levels of apigenin, an antioxidant that helps soothe and calm irritated skin.
Seeds: Chamomile seeds are used to make essential oils, which can help ease nipple pain during breastfeeding by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest matricaria chamomilla flowers at peak bloom, allowing for proper drying within 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried flowers in a clevenger apparatus using 100 grams of flowers per liter of water.
Monitor and record the yield and quality of the essential oil as it collects over several hours.
Filter the resulting oil through activated charcoal or diatomaceous earth to remove impurities and sediment.
Transfer the final product, which should be a pale yellowish liquid with a calming aroma, into dark glass bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice essential oils helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because it has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated nipples. The oil's warming sensation also increases blood flow to the area, reducing swelling and discomfort. Additionally, licorice root is known for its ability to stimulate milk production and aid in the let-down reflex, making it a valuable addition to any post-pumping or breastfeeding routine. As a result, using herbal licorice essential oils can provide instant relief from nipple pain, allowing new mothers to focus on bonding with their babies without distraction.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice root has anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce swelling and redness, providing relief from nipple soreness and discomfort caused by breastfeeding.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of licorice root essential oil may help combat bacterial or fungal infections that can cause nipple pain, infection, or inflammation during breastfeeding.

Anodyne: Licorice root has been traditionally used as an anodyne, meaning it can relieve pain and discomfort without numbing the area, making it a gentle and effective solution for nipple pain relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to soothe and calm irritated skin.
Barks: Licorice bark is used to create an essential oil that can help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the nipple area, making it an effective remedy for nipple pain during breastfeeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried licorice root in a clean environment then grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 10 grams of powdered licorice root with 100 milliliters of high-quality carrier oil like sweet almond or coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60-70 degrees celsius for 30 minutes stirring occasionally to facilitate extraction.
Allow the mixture to steep for an additional 2 hours then strain it through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Transfer the infused oil to a dark glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or until ready to use.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat nipple pain during breastfeeding with herbal essential oils is to apply a few drops of lavender oil, known for its calming properties, directly to the affected area. Alternatively, blend peppermint oil with coconut or sweet almond oil to create a soothing topical treatment that can help reduce inflammation and discomfort.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to nipple pain during breastfeeding that are treated with herbal essential oils are conditions such as mastitis, engorgement, and thrush. Tea tree oil has antibacterial properties that can help combat mastitis-causing bacteria, while peppermint oil can relieve discomfort associated with engorgement. Lavender oil and coconut oil have antifungal properties that can soothe and treat thrush infections, providing relief to breastfeeding mothers.
